commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=7bef412e6fd1b7460869ee033b40ccdeda7ccbac)
commands.review_summary:
The PR adds a stranded `message_tool_only` reply guard/logger, wires it into `runReplyAgent`, and adds unit/integration coverage plus a qa-channel scenario.

PR surface: Source +82, Tests +143, Docs +78. Total +303 across 5 files.

Reproducibility: yes. The source path is clear: `message_tool_only` suppresses automatic final delivery, and a non-empty final payload with no successful message-tool delivery is currently dropped from the source channel.
commands.review_followup: Manual review is needed because the remaining blocker combines a product decision about warn-only mitigation with contributor or maintainer log-backed proof; ClawSweeper should not auto-repair without that proof.

target.title: fix(auto-reply): warn on stranded message_tool_only final reply (#85714)
target.body:
AI-assisted (Claude Code). đź¤–

## Summary

Under `messages.visibleReplies: "message_tool"`, OpenClaw keeps the agent's final assistant text **private** and delivers visible output only through explicit `message(action=send)` calls. When the model occasionally omits the delivery tool on its final turn and emits the reply as plain text (instead of the `NO_REPLY` sentinel), that real reply is **silently dropped** â€” the user sees an unresponsive bot even though the turn completed normally (#85714).

This patch makes the loss **observable** without changing the delivery contract. When a `message_tool_only` turn produces a real final reply but nothing was delivered this turn, the gateway logs a WARN. It deliberately does **not** auto-deliver the text: that would defeat the documented "keep normal final text private" guarantee of `visibleReplies: message_tool` and risk double-delivery or leaking internal-only content.

Refs #85714. The issue is tagged `needs-product-decision` / `needs-maintainer-review`; this PR proposes the conservative **non-silent-signal** option (observe, don't auto-deliver) rather than changing delivery semantics.

## Changes

- `src/auto-reply/reply/stranded-source-reply.ts` (new): `isStrandedMessageToolReply` (pure guard) and `warnStrandedMessageToolReply` (logs `sessionKey` / `channel` / char count only â€” never the response body).
- `src/auto-reply/reply/agent-runner.ts`: at the `message_tool_only` suppression seam, warn when the final reply is real, non-sentinel, not policy-denied, and nothing was delivered.
- `src/auto-reply/reply/stranded-source-reply.test.ts` (new) and a `runReplyAgent` integration case asserting the WARN fires (and stays silent when the tool delivered).
- `qa/scenarios/channels/message-tool-stranded-final-reply.md` (new): qa-channel repro scenario.

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: A real final reply under `messages.visibleReplies: message_tool` is silently dropped (nothing reaches the channel) when the model does not call the delivery tool; after this patch the running gateway emits an observable WARN instead of failing silently.

Real environment tested: A real OpenClaw gateway process driven through the bundled QA harness on macOS â€” a live child gateway plus the `qa-channel` transport. The agent runs the normal `runReplyAgent` delivery pipeline; only the model backend is a deterministic local server (the local install has no provider credentials, so a live cloud model could not be exercised).

Exact steps or command run after this patch: `pnpm openclaw qa suite --scenario message-tool-stranded-final-reply --provider-mode mock-openai`

Evidence after fix: copied live console output from the real gateway run:

```console
$ pnpm openclaw qa suite --scenario message-tool-stranded-final-reply --provider-mode mock-openai
QA suite watch: http://127.0.0.1:49981
QA suite report: .artifacts/qa-e2e/stranded-85714/qa-suite-report.md
QA suite summary: .artifacts/qa-e2e/stranded-85714/qa-suite-summary.json

(qa-suite-report.md) Status: pass
```

Runtime log line emitted by the gateway on the stranded turn (source: `source-reply/stranded`):

```console
[source-reply/stranded] agent produced a final reply but never called the configured delivery tool (message_tool_only); response kept private and not delivered to the source channel
```

Observed result after fix: the direct turn produced a real final reply, the gateway delivered nothing to the originating conversation (the stranding from #85714 is reproduced â€” zero outbound), and the gateway emitted the WARN above. When the same turn does deliver through the message tool, no WARN is emitted.

What was not tested: a live cloud-model run against a real chat transport (Telegram/etc.) â€” the local install has no provider/model credentials, so the gateway ran against the deterministic local model server. The literal WARN line was not pulled from the QA gateway's own on-disk log because its run home is ephemeral; the WARN emission is otherwise confirmed by the `runReplyAgent` integration case exercising the real suppression branch. A maintainer may apply `proof: override` if a live-transport run is required before merge.

## Verification (supplemental)

- Unit + integration tests: `pnpm test src/auto-reply/reply/stranded-source-reply.test.ts src/auto-reply/reply/agent-runner.misc.runreplyagent.test.ts` â€” all pass.
- `pnpm tsgo:core` / `pnpm tsgo:core:test` exit 0; oxlint 0/0; oxfmt clean; `pnpm build` exit 0.
- Rebased on latest `upstream/main`.
